question:
Why is the "just-world hypothesis" — the belief that people always get what they deserve — considered a cognitive bias rather than an accurate model of reality?
A) It is a purely mathematical theory with no social applications.
B) It leads people to blame victims for their misfortune, as this preserves a comforting but inaccurate belief that the world is fundamentally fair.
C) The world has been statistically proven to be perfectly just.
D) It only applies to people who have never experienced hardship.
